content.content:
1→
     2→Proposta: Server Driven UI (SDUI): Um Padrão para Telas Parametrizáveis
     3→Status: Rascunho
     4→Versão: 1.0
     5→
     6→
     7→                              Resumo
     8→
     9→   Esta proposta descreve uma forma de estruturar o conteúdo das
    10→   telas parametrizáveis (vouchers, benefícios, resgates, etc)
    11→   de forma padronizada para reduzir a necessidade de deploy quando
    12→   há mudanças de texto, formatação ou ordem de elementos.
    13→
    14→   A proposta é baseada em padrões utilizados por empresas como
    15→   Airbnb, Nubank e DoorDash, adaptados para nossa realidade.
    16→
    17→
    18→Índice
    19→
    20→   1. Contexto
    21→   2. Proposta
    22→   3. Como Funciona
    23→      3.1. Estrutura de Tela
    24→      3.2. Componentes
    25→      3.3. Conteúdo Rico
    26→      3.4. Valores e Contexto
    27→      3.5. Regras e Condicionais
    28→   4. Exemplo: Tela de Voucher
    29→   5. Implementação
    30→   6. Referências
    31→
    32→
    33→1. Contexto
    34→
    35→   Muitas aplicações possuem telas parametrizáveis, funcionando como 
    36→   templates, que recebem dados do backend, definidos conforme a 
    37→   necessidade de cada template. Na prática, os valores são dinâmicos,
    38→   mas a estrutura não. A cada nova necessidade de alteração simples de
    39→   layout e formatação, como destacar um texto em negrito, um novo campo é
    40→   adicionado no JSON de resposta do servidor e uma nova regra condicional
    41→   (if) é adicionada no frontend, resultando em armazenamento de dados legados
    42→   e no acúmulo de código morto.
    43→
    44→2. Proposta: Padrão SDUI.
    45→    
    46→    É um padrão arquitetural no qual o backend define a lógica de exibição 
    47→    dos dados, indo além da definição de parâmetros textuais.
    48→
    49→    Empresas que usam esse padrão:
    50→
    51→        - Nubank: 70% das novas telas usam esse formato
    52→        - Airbnb: Busca, listagens e checkout
    53→        - DoorDash: Reduziu tempo de mudanças de semanas para horas
    54→
    55→3. Como Funciona
    56→
    57→3.1. Estrutura de Tela
    58→
    59→     Uma tela é composta por:
    60→
    61→     {
    62→       "screen": {
    63→         "id": "voucher_form",
    64→         "context": { ... },      // dados para usar nos componentes
    65→         "header": { ... },       // cabeçalho
    66→         "body": [ ... ],         // array de componentes
    67→         "footer": { ... }        // rodapé (botão de ação)
    68→       }
    69→     }
    70→
    71→     O body é um array. A ordem do array é a ordem na tela.
    72→
    73→3.2. Componentes
    74→
    75→     Cada componente tem um tipo e propriedades:
    76→
    77→     { "type": "heading", "props": { "text": "Título" } }
    78→     { "type": "input", "props": { "label": "Valor em pontos" } }
    79→     { "type": "button", "props": { "label": "Continuar" } }
    80→
    81→     O app tem um registro de componentes:
    82→
    83→     const Componentes = {
    84→       'heading': HeadingComponent,
    85→       'input': InputComponent,
    86→       'button': ButtonComponent,
    87→       'balance': BalanceComponent,
    88→       // ...
    89→     };
    90→
    91→     Quando recebe o JSON, renderiza cada item do array usando
    92→     o componente correspondente ao tipo.
    93→
    94→     Tipos que já temos no Design System podem ser mapeados
    95→     diretamente. Não precisa criar componentes novos.
    96→
    97→3.3. Conteúdo Rico
    98→
    99→     Para textos que precisam de formatação inline (negrito,
   100→     links, ícones), usamos uma estrutura de "nodes":
   101→
   102→     {
   103→       "type": "rich_text",
   104→       "children": [
   105→         { "type": "text", "value": "Confira os " },
   106→         { "type": "text", "value": "termos", "bold": true },
   107→         { "type": "text", "value": " ou " },
   108→         { "type": "link", "text": "fale conosco", "to": "app://suporte" }
   109→       ]
   110→     }
   111→
   112→     Por que não markdown?
   113→
   114→     Se usarmos [texto](url) para links e depois precisarmos
   115→     adicionar tracking ou confirmação, temos que refatorar o
   116→     parser.
   117→
   118→     Com nodes, é só adicionar uma propriedade:
   119→
   120→     {
   121→       "type": "link",
   122→       "text": "fale conosco",
   123→       "to": "app://suporte",
   124→       "trackEvent": "suporte_click"    // adicionado depois
   125→     }
   126→
   127→3.4. Valores e Contexto
   128→
   129→
   130→     O Backend envia os valores no campo context:
   131→     {
   132→       "context": {
   133→         "balance": {
   134→           "points": 25720,
   135→           "value": 7716.00
   136→         }
   137→       }
   138→     }
   139→
   140→
   141→     Para textos que dependem do contexto, usamos interpolação:
   142→
   143→     { "type": "heading", "props": { "text": "Voucher para {{product.name}}" } }
   144→
   145→     O app substitui {{product.name}} pelo valor do contexto.
   146→
   147→3.5. Regras e Condicionais
   148→
   149→     Para controlar visibilidade e habilitação, usamos regras
   150→     simples baseadas em JSON Logic:
   151→
   152→     Botão habilitado só quando valor > 0:
   153→
   154→     {
   155→       "type": "button",
   156→       "props": {
   157→         "label": "Continuar",
   158→         "enabled": {
   159→           "rule": { ">": [{ "var": "form.points" }, 0] }
   160→         }
   161→       }
   162→     }
   163→
   164→     Os operadores suportados são básicos:
   165→     - var (acesso a dados)
   166→     - ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparação)
   167→     - and, or, ! (lógica)
   168→
   169→     Não tem loops, não tem eval, não tem risco.
   170→
   171→     Validação de inputs segue o mesmo padrão:
   172→
   173→     {
   174→       "type": "input",
   175→       "props": {
   176→         "id": "points",
   177→         "validation": {
   178→           "rules": [
   179→             {
   180→               "rule": { ">": [{ "var": "value" }, 0] },
   181→               "message": "Informe um valor maior que zero"
   182→             }
   183→           ]
   184→         }
   185→       }
   186→     }
   187→
   188→5. Exemplo: Tela de Voucher
   189→
   190→   Estrutura completa para a tela de voucher:
   191→
   192→   {
   193→     "screen": {
   194→       "id": "voucher_form",
   195→       "context": {
   196→         "product": {
   197→           "key": "abastecimento",
   198→           "name": "Abastecimento"
   199→         },
   200→         "balance": {
   201→           "points": 25720,
   202→           "pointsFormatted": "25.720",
   203→           "valueFormatted": "R$ 7.716,00"
   204→         }
   205→       },
   206→       "header": {
   207→         "type": "nav_header",
   208→         "props": {
   209→           "title": "Voucher para {{product.name}}",
   210→           "showBack": true
   211→         }
   212→       },
   213→       "body": [
   214→         {
   215→           "type": "heading",
   216→           "props": { "text": "Defina o desconto para seu {{product.key}}" }
   217→         },
   218→         {
   219→           "type": "balance_display",
   220→           "props": {
   221→             "label": "Pontos disponíveis",
   222→             "points": "{{balance.pointsFormatted}}",
   223→             "value": "{{balance.valueFormatted}}"
   224→           }
   225→         },
   226→         {
   227→           "type": "points_input",
   228→           "props": {
   229→             "id": "points",
   230→             "label": "Valor em pontos",
   231→             "type": "numeric"
   232→           }
   233→         },
   234→         {
   235→           "type": "points_input",
   236→           "props": {
   237→             "id": "reais",
   238→             "label": "Valor em reais",
   239→             "type": "currency"
   240→           }
   241→         },
   242→         {
   243→           "type": "link",
   244→           "props": {
   245→             "text": "Exibir regras de uso",
   246→             "to": "app://voucher/rules"
   247→           }
   248→         }
   249→       ],
   250→       "footer": {
   251→         "type": "button",
   252→         "props": {
   253→           "label": "Continuar",
   254→           "style": "primary",
   255→           "enabled": {
   256→             "rule": { ">": [{ "var": "form.points" }, 0] }
   257→           }
   258→         }
   259→       }
   260→     }
   261→   }
   262→
   263→   O mesmo código renderiza Abastecimento, Estacionamento,
   264→   Seguro Auto e IPVA. Mudando apenas o context, não o app.
   265→
   266→
   267→6. Implementação
   268→
   269→   6.1. O que precisa ser feito
   270→
   271→        - Registro de componentes (mapeamento tipo ‣ componente)
   272→        - Renderizador que itera o array e renderiza cada tipo
   273→        - Parser de interpolação (substituir {{var}})
   274→        - Avaliador de regras (subset de JSON Logic)
   275→
   276→   6.2. O que não muda
   277→
   278→        - Design System continua o mesmo
   279→        - Componentes existentes são reutilizados
   280→        - Telas atuais continuam funcionando
   281→        - Backend continua sendo Lambda que recebe/devolve JSON
   282→
   283→   6.3. Escopo inicial
   284→
   285→        Implementar na tela de Voucher (SPMAIS-34018) que já está
   286→        com feature toggle. É o cenário ideal:
   287→
   288→        - Feature nova, sem legado
   289→        - 4 variações do mesmo fluxo
   290→        - Retrabalho previsível (produto vai pedir ajustes)
   291→
   292→   6.4. Validação
   293→
   294→        O JSON pode ser validado antes de salvar no backend e
   295→        antes de renderizar no app. Componentes desconhecidos
   296→        usam fallback. Regras inválidas retornam false.
   297→
   298→7. Referências
   299→
   300→   [AIRBNB]    "A Deep Dive into Airbnb's Server-Driven UI System"
   301→               https://medium.com/airbnb-engineering/a-deep-dive-into-airbnbs-server-driven-ui-system-842244c5f5
   302→
   303→   [NUBANK]    "Backend Driven Content at Nubank"
   304→               https://building.nubank.com/server-driven-ui-framework-at-nubank/
   305→
   306→   [JSONLOGIC] JSON Logic - Regras em JSON
   307→               https://jsonlogic.com/
   308→
   309→

content.text: Now let me check the progress file to understand what's been done:

content.content:
1→# SP_0001.0 — Setup Storybook
     2→- [COMPLETED] Configure Storybook so this MFE can render screens independently of the SuperApp.
     3→
     4→Context:
     5→- This is a React Native MFE (package.json: react 18.2.0, react-native 0.73.6)
     6→- The design system (@semparar/design-system-app ^3.39.15) already has Storybook
     7→  configured — its setup can be used as reference if the executing agent needs it
     8→- Babel aliases: ~ → ./src/ (babel.config.js line 27)
     9→- TS alias: ~/store → ./@types/store.d.ts (tsconfig.json line 11) — this is a
    10→  module augmentation for the SuperApp's Zustand store, needs mocking
    11→- DS components depend on design tokens — may need a ThemeProvider or similar
    12→
    13→Deliverable: `npm run storybook` opens a browser with a smoke test story that
    14→renders at least one DS component (e.g., Button or Text from @semparar/design-system-app).
    15→
    16→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→
    18→# SP_0001.1 — Validate DS components in Storybook
    19→- [IN_PROGRESS] Prove that the design system components actually used in voucher screens
    20→      render correctly in the Storybook web context.
    21→
    22→Context:
    23→- The voucher screens use these DS components: Text, Button, Box, InputBox,
    24→  Alert, Icon, ListTextIcon, SeparatorHorizontal, BackDropBottom, Loading
    25→- See existing usage in:
    26→  - src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/view.tsx (form with inputs, balance, button)
    27→  - src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/view.tsx (review, success, error states)
    28→  - src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/index.tsx (list items with icons)
    29→- Some components may not render in web — document which ones work and which don't
    30→
    31→Deliverable: A story page rendering each DS component listed above. Annotate any
    32→that fail or need workarounds.
    33→
    34→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    35→
    36→# SP_0002.0 — SDUI Core: Types & Interfaces
    37→- [COMPLETED] Define the TypeScript type system for the SDUI architecture described in
    38→      .claude/rfc-conteudo-dinamico.txt (sections 3.1–3.5).
    39→
    40→Context:
    41→- Screen structure: { id, context, header, body[], footer } (RFC section 3.1)
    42→- Components: { type, props } (RFC section 3.2)
    43→- Rich text: { type: "rich_text", children: [nodes] } (RFC section 3.3)
    44→- Context interpolation: {{var}} pattern (RFC section 3.4)
    45→- Conditionals: JSON Logic subset (RFC section 3.5)
    46→- Follow project conventions: .claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md (services architecture,
    47→  models in Models/ folders)
    48→
    49→Deliverable: Type definitions in src/services/sdui/models/ that contract the
    50→entire SDUI system (Screen, Component, Context, RichText, ComponentRegistry).
    51→
    52→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    53→
    54→# SP_0002.1 — SDUI Core: Interpolation Parser
    55→- [ ] Implement the {{var}} replacement engine from RFC section 3.4.
    56→
    57→Context:
    58→- Must resolve nested paths: {{product.name}}, {{balance.pointsFormatted}}
    59→- Must deep-walk an object interpolating all string values in props
    60→- Missing values should resolve gracefully (empty string, not crash)
    61→- Existing interpolation example in RFC:
    62→  "Voucher para {{product.name}}" + context.product.name = "Abastecimento"
    63→  → "Voucher para Abastecimento"
    64→- Types from SP_0002.0 should be used
    65→
    66→Deliverable: Interpolation utility with unit tests.
    67→
    68→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    69→
    70→# SP_0002.2 — SDUI Core: Component Registry & Screen Renderer
    71→- [ ] Build the rendering engine that turns a JSON screen definition into React
    72→      components. This is the core of the SDUI architecture (RFC sections 3.1–3.2).
    73→
    74→Context:
    75→- Registry maps string type → React component (RFC section 3.2 "Componentes")
    76→- Renderer receives SDUIScreen JSON, iterates body[], renders each via registry
    77→- Must apply interpolation (SP_0002.1) to all string props using screen context
    78→- Unknown types should render a visible fallback (not crash)
    79→- Types from SP_0002.0, interpolation from SP_0002.1
    80→
    81→Deliverable: A <ScreenRenderer screen={json} /> component that renders a
    82→screen definition from JSON.
    83→
    84→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    85→
    86→# SP_0002.3 — SDUI Component Adapters
    87→- [ ] Create adapters that bridge SDUI component types to actual DS components.
    88→
    89→Context:
    90→- Each adapter maps SDUI props to @semparar/design-system-app component props
    91→- Required adapters based on voucher screen analysis (TASK.pdf + existing screens):
    92→
    93→  | SDUI type        | DS component(s)           | Used in                    |
    94→  |------------------|---------------------------|----------------------------|
    95→  | heading          | Text (headingMDL)         | Form title, Review title   |
    96→  | text             | Text (configurable)       | Labels, descriptions       |
    97→  | balance_display  | Box + Text                | "Pontos disponíveis: X"   |
    98→  | points_input     | InputBox                  | Points/reais fields        |
    99→  | button           | Button                    | CTA buttons                |
   100→  | link             | Text (pressable)          | "Exibir regras de uso"     |
   101→  | alert            | Alert                     | Info boxes in summary      |
   102→  | list_text_icon   | ListTextIcon              | Onboarding items, rules    |
   103→  | separator        | SeparatorHorizontal       | Between fields             |
   104→  | icon_text_row    | Box + Icon + Text         | Regras de uso bullet items |
   105→  | section_heading  | Text (subtitleXXL)        | Regras section titles      |
   106→
   107→- Register all in the default registry from SP_0002.2
   108→- DS component validation from SP_0001.1 may affect which adapters are viable
   109→
   110→Deliverable: All voucher-relevant adapters registered in the component registry.
   111→
   112→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   113→
   114→# SP_0002.4 — Mock JSON Payloads
   115→- [ ] Create realistic JSON payloads for the voucher screens described in TASK.pdf.
   116→
   117→Context:
   118→- 4 screens need payloads (see TASK.pdf pages 2–4 for visual reference):
   119→  1. Voucher form (points/reais input) — reference: InvoiceCashbackPoints/view.tsx
   120→  2. Voucher summary (review before confirm) — reference: InvoiceCashbackSummary/view.tsx lines 47-151
   121→  3. Voucher conclusion (success) — reference: InvoiceCashbackSummary/view.tsx lines 154-203
   122→  4. Regras de uso (rules) — reference: TASK.pdf page 4 screenshot
   123→- Product codes (src/screens/Points/data/index.ts lines 62-70):
   124→  206 = Abastecimento, 205 = Estacionamento, 207 = Seguro Auto, 208 = IPVA
   125→- Text differences per product visible in TASK.pdf page 2:
   126→  - Cashback: "Defina quanto cashback quer na sua fatura"
   127→  - Voucher: "Defina o desconto para seu abastecimento"
   128→  - Cashback: "Cashback aplicado" / Voucher: "O voucher foi criado"
   129→  - Cashback: "Confirmar desconto" / Voucher: "Criar voucher"
   130→- Static content (labels, titles, rules) goes in JSON. Dynamic data (balance,
   131→  points) comes from context — not hardcoded in the payload.
   132→- Include at least 2 product variants (Abastecimento + Estacionamento) to prove
   133→  the same renderer handles different products via different JSON.
   134→
   135→Deliverable: JSON payloads for 4 screens × 2 products.
   136→
   137→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   138→
   139→# SP_0002.5 — Demo Stories: SDUI Rendering
   140→- [ ] Final demonstration — render all voucher screens from JSON through the SDUI
   141→      engine in Storybook.
   142→
   143→Context:
   144→- Uses: ScreenRenderer (SP_0002.2), adapters (SP_0002.3), payloads (SP_0002.4)
   145→- Each story feeds a mock JSON payload into <ScreenRenderer />
   146→- Key demonstration: switching product context (Abastecimento → Estacionamento)
   147→  changes the rendered screen WITHOUT changing any code — only JSON differs
   148→- This is the spike's central proof: same code, different JSON, different screens
   149→
   150→Deliverable: Storybook stories showing all 4 voucher screens rendered from JSON,
   151→with product variant toggle proving reusability. This is the spike's final output.
   152→

content.content:
1→import React, { useState, useRef } from 'react';
     2→import { View, ScrollView } from 'react-native';
     3→import {
     4→  Text,
     5→  Button,
     6→  Box,
     7→  InputBox,
     8→  Alert,
     9→  Icon,
    10→  ListTextIcon,
    11→  SeparatorHorizontal,
    12→  BackDropBottom,
    13→  Loading,
    14→} from '@semparar/design-system-app';
    15→import type { Meta, StoryObj } from 'storybook/internal/types';
    16→
    17→function StatusBadge({ status }: { status: 'ok' | 'fail' | 'warn' }) {
    18→  const colors = { ok: '#22c55e', fail: '#ef4444', warn: '#f59e0b' };
    19→  const labels = { ok: 'RENDERS', fail: 'FAILS', warn: 'PARTIAL' };
    20→  return (
    21→    <View
    22→      style={{
    23→        backgroundColor: colors[status],
    24→        paddingHorizontal: 8,
    25→        paddingVertical: 2,
    26→        borderRadius: 4,
    27→        alignSelf: 'flex-start',
    28→        marginBottom: 8,
    29→      }}
    30→    >
    31→      <Text type="bodySM" color="INTENSE_COLOR_01">
    32→        {labels[status]}
    33→      </Text>
    34→    </View>
    35→  );
    36→}
    37→
    38→function Section({
    39→  title,
    40→  children,
    41→}: {
    42→  title: string;
    43→  children: React.ReactNode;
    44→}) {
    45→  return (
    46→    <View style={{ marginBottom: 32 }}>
    47→      <Text type="subtitleXXL" mb="smaller">
    48→        {title}
    49→      </Text>
    50→      <Box
    51→        pd="squishSmall"
    52→        bgColor="LIGHT_BASE_02"
    53→        br="sm"
    54→        bw="thin"
    55→        borderColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_02"
    56→      >
    57→        {children}
    58→      </Box>
    59→    </View>
    60→  );
    61→}
    62→
    63→function TextValidation() {
    64→  return (
    65→    <Section title="1. Text">
    66→      <StatusBadge status="ok" />
    67→      <Text type="headingMDL" mb="nano">
    68→        headingMDL — Form titles
    69→      </Text>
    70→      <Text type="subtitleXXL" mb="nano">
    71→        subtitleXXL — Section headings
    72→      </Text>
    73→      <Text type="bodyMD" mb="nano">
    74→        bodyMD — Body text
    75→      </Text>
    76→      <Text type="paragraphMD" mb="nano">
    77→        paragraphMD — Descriptions
    78→      </Text>
    79→      <Text type="bodySM" mb="nano" color="INTENSE_COLOR_03">
    80→        bodySM + color — Secondary text
    81→      </Text>
    82→      <Text
    83→        type="bodyMD"
    84→        color="PRYMARY_BASE_02"
    85→        underline
    86→        onPress={() => {}}
    87→      >
    88→        Pressable + underline — Link style
    89→      </Text>
    90→    </Section>
    91→  );
    92→}
    93→
    94→function ButtonValidation() {
    95→  return (
    96→    <Section title="2. Button">
    97→      <StatusBadge status="ok" />
    98→      <Button text="Primary Large (default)" onPress={() => {}} mb="nano" />
    99→      <Button
   100→        text="Disabled State"
   101→        onPress={() => {}}
   102→        disabled
   103→        mb="nano"
   104→      />
   105→      <Button
   106→        text="With Icon"
   107→        onPress={() => {}}
   108→        icon="icon-check"
   109→        mb="nano"
   110→      />
   111→      <Button
   112→        text="Loading State"
   113→        onPress={() => {}}
   114→        isLoading

   119→
   120→function BoxValidation() {
   121→  return (
   122→    <Section title="3. Box">
   123→      <StatusBadge status="ok" />
   124→      <Box
   125→        pd="squishSmall"
   126→        bgColor="PRYMARY_BASE_01"
   127→        br="md"
   128→        mb="nano"
   129→      >

   131→          Box with bgColor, padding, borderRadius

   135→        <Box
   136→          pd="squishSmall"
   137→          bgColor="LIGHT_BASE_02"
   138→          br="sm"
   139→          mr="nano"
   140→          flex={1}
   141→        >

   143→        </Box>
   144→        <Box pd="squishSmall" bgColor="LIGHT_BASE_02" br="sm" flex={1}>

   149→        pd="innerMedium"

   159→
   160→function InputBoxValidation() {
   161→  const [value1, setValue1] = useState('');
   162→  const [value2, setValue2] = useState('');
   163→  const [value3, setValue3] = useState('');
   164→
   165→  return (
   166→    <Section title="4. InputBox">

   187→      />
   188→      <InputBox
   189→        label="With error"

   192→        value={value3}
   193→        onChangeText={setValue3}
   194→        error
   195→        textError="Valor excede o saldo disponível"
   196→      />

   200→
   201→function AlertValidation() {
   202→  return (
   203→    <Section title="5. Alert">
   204→      <StatusBadge status="ok" />
   205→      <Alert
   206→        label="O valor é aplicado e exibido nos lançamentos da sua fatura aberta."
   207→        type="highlight"

   210→      />

   216→      />
   217→      <Alert
   218→        label="Erro ao processar sua solicitação."

   231→
   232→function IconValidation() {
   233→  return (
   234→    <Section title="6. Icon">
   235→      <StatusBadge status="ok" />
   236→      <Box direction align="center" mb="nano">
   237→        <Icon name="icon-check" size="md" color="PRYMARY_BASE_02" mr="nano" />
   238→        <Icon
   239→          name="icon-calendar"
   240→          size="md"
   241→          color="BRAND_BASE_01"
   242→          mr="nano"
   243→        />

   245→        <Icon
   246→          name="icon-switch-vertical"
   247→          size="md"
   248→          color="INTENSE_COLOR_03"
   249→        />
   250→      </Box>

   252→        <Icon
   253→          name="icon-check"
   254→          size="sm"
   255→          color="BRAND_BASE_01"
   256→          hasBackground
   257→          backgroundType="circle"
   258→          bgColorCircle="INTENSE_COLOR_01"

   260→          mr="nano"
   261→        />
   262→        <Text type="bodySM">Icon with circle background</Text>
   263→      </Box>

   267→
   268→function ListTextIconValidation() {
   269→  return (
   270→    <Section title="7. ListTextIcon">
   271→      <StatusBadge status="ok" />
   272→      <ListTextIcon
   273→        items={[
   274→          {
   275→            icon: 'icon-switch-vertical',
   276→            iconColor: 'BRAND_BASE_01',
   277→            iconColorShape: 'INTENSE_COLOR_01',
   278→            description: 'Cada 100 pontos valem R$ 1,00 no voucher',
   279→            hasBackgroundIcon: true,
   280→          },
   281→          {
   282→            icon: 'icon-calendar',
   283→            iconColor: 'BRAND_BASE_01',
   284→            iconColorShape: 'INTENSE_COLOR_01',
   285→            description: 'Você define quanto desconto quer no abastecimento',
   286→            hasBackgroundIcon: true,
   287→          },
   288→          {
   289→            icon: 'icon-check',
   290→            iconColor: 'BRAND_BASE_01',
   291→            iconColorShape: 'INTENSE_COLOR_01',
   292→            description: 'Após o resgate, o voucher fica disponível no app',
   293→            hasBackgroundIcon: true,
   294→          },
   295→        ]}
   296→        type="list"
   297→      />
   298→    </Section>

   300→}
   301→
   302→function SeparatorValidation() {
   303→  return (
   304→    <Section title="8. SeparatorHorizontal">
   305→      <StatusBadge status="ok" />
   306→      <Text type="bodySM" mb="nano">
   307→        Content before separator
   308→      </Text>
   309→      <SeparatorHorizontal
   310→        mb="nano"
   311→        mt="nano"
   312→        bgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_02"
   313→        height={1}
   314→        width="100%"
   315→      />
   316→      <Text type="bodySM">Content after separator</Text>
   317→    </Section>
   318→  );
   319→}
   320→
   321→function BackDropBottomValidation() {
   322→  return (
   323→    <Section title="9. BackDropBottom">
   324→      <StatusBadge status="warn" />
   325→      <Text type="bodySM" color="INTENSE_COLOR_03" mb="nano">
   326→        BackDropBottom depends on react-native-modalize which requires native
   327→        modules (GestureHandler, Animated). It will NOT render in the Storybook
   328→        web context. Use a plain View/ScrollView wrapper as substitute for
   329→        SDUI stories.
   330→      </Text>
   331→    </Section>
   332→  );
   333→}
   334→
   335→function LoadingValidation() {
   336→  return (
   337→    <Section title="10. Loading">
   338→      <StatusBadge status="warn" />
   339→      <Text type="bodySM" color="INTENSE_COLOR_03" mb="nano">
   340→        Loading depends on lottie-react-native which requires native Lottie
   341→        bindings. It will NOT render in the Storybook web context. Use a
   342→        simple ActivityIndicator or Text fallback for SDUI stories.
   343→      </Text>
   344→    </Section>
   345→  );
   346→}
   347→
   348→function DSComponentValidation() {
   349→  return (
   350→    <ScrollView style={{ flex: 1 }}>
   351→      <View style={{ padding: 24, maxWidth: 480 }}>
   352→        <Text type="headingMDL" mb="smaller">
   353→          DS Component Validation — SP_0001.1
   354→        </Text>
   355→        <Text type="bodyMD" mb="smaller" color="INTENSE_COLOR_03">
   356→          Validates all @semparar/design-system-app components used in voucher
   357→          screens. Each section shows the component with realistic props matching
   358→          InvoiceCashback screen usage.
   359→        </Text>
   360→        <SeparatorHorizontal
   361→          mb="smaller"
   362→          bgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_02"
   363→          height={1}
   364→          width="100%"
   365→        />
   366→        <TextValidation />
   367→        <ButtonValidation />
   368→        <BoxValidation />
   369→        <InputBoxValidation />
   370→        <AlertValidation />
   371→        <IconValidation />
   372→        <ListTextIconValidation />
   373→        <SeparatorValidation />
   374→        <BackDropBottomValidation />
   375→        <LoadingValidation />
   376→
   377→        <Box pd="squishSmall" bgColor="LIGHT_BASE_02" br="sm" mt="smaller">
   378→          <Text type="subtitleXXL" mb="nano">
   379→            Summary
   380→          </Text>
   381→          <Text type="bodyMD" mb="nano">
   382→            8/10 components render correctly in Storybook web context.
   383→          </Text>
   384→          <Text type="bodySM" color="INTENSE_COLOR_03">
   385→            BackDropBottom (Modalize) and Loading (Lottie) require native modules
   386→            and cannot render on web. SDUI adapters should provide web-safe
   387→            fallbacks for these two components.
   388→          </Text>
   389→        </Box>
   390→      </View>
   391→    </ScrollView>
   392→  );
   393→}
   394→
   395→const meta: Meta<typeof DSComponentValidation> = {
   396→  title: 'Validation/DS Components',
   397→  component: DSComponentValidation,
   398→};
   399→
   400→export default meta;
   401→
   402→type Story = StoryObj<typeof meta>;
   403→
   404→export const AllComponents: Story = {};
   405→

content.content:
1→# Frontend Style Guide — Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
     2→
     3→Welcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
     4→This version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
     5→
     6→> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. ☕
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## :pushpin: Table of Contents
    11→
    12→- [Introduction](#introduction)
    13→- [Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
    14→- [Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
    15→- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
    16→- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
    17→- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
    18→- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
    19→- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
    20→- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
    21→- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
    22→- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
    23→- [Checklist](#checklist)
    24→
    25→---
    26→
    27→## Introduction
    28→
    29→The goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.
    30→
    31→Key decisions:
    32→
    33→- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
    34→- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
    35→- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
    36→- Cache is **typed and reusable**.
    37→
    38→---
    39→
    40→## Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern
    41→
    42→Each screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
    43→
    44→```
    45→screens/Home/
    46→├── index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
    47→├── view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
    48→└── Models/
    49→    └── index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
    50→```
    51→
    52→**Models/index.d.ts**
    53→
    54→```ts
    55→export interface HomeParams {
    56→  welcome?: string;
    57→}
    58→export interface HomeState {
    59→  greeting: string;
    60→  isLoading: boolean;
    61→}
    62→export interface HomeHandlers {
    63→  onPressCTA: () => void;
    64→}
    65→export type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
    66→```
    67→
    68→**index.ts (logic hook)**
    69→
    70→```ts
    71→import { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from 'react';
    72→import type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from './Models';
    73→import { useUserStore } from '@/services/user/store'; // Zustand slice example
    74→
    75→export function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
    76→  const name = useUserStore(s => s.name);
    77→  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    78→
    79→  useEffect(() => {
    80→    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
    81→    return () => clearTimeout(t);
    82→  }, []);
    83→
    84→  const greeting = useMemo(
    85→    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || 'guest'}!`,
    86→    [
    87→      params?.welcome,
    88→      name,
    89→    ],
    90→  );
    91→
    92→  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
    93→    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
    94→  }, []);
    95→
    96→  return {
    97→    greeting,
    98→    isLoading,
    99→    onPressCTA,
   100→  };
   101→}
   102→```
   103→
   104→**view.tsx (render)**
   105→
   106→```tsx
   107→import React from 'react';
   108→import { View } from 'react-native';
   109→import { Text, Button } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
   110→import { useHomeScreen } from './index';
   111→import type { HomeParams } from './Models';
   112→
   113→type Props = HomeParams;
   114→
   115→export default function HomeScreen(props: Props) {
   116→  const { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA } = useHomeScreen(props);
   117→
   118→  if (isLoading) {
   119→    return (
   120→      <View>
   121→        <Text>Loading...</Text>
   122→      </View>
   123→    );
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  return (
   127→    <View>
   128→      <Text>{greeting}</Text>
   129→      <Button onPress={onPressCTA} text="Continue" />
   130→    </View>
   131→  );
   132→}
   133→```
   134→
   135→---
   136→
   137→## Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern
   138→
   139→```
   140→atomic/atoms/Button/
   141→├── index.ts        # logic: export useButton()
   142→├── view.tsx        # render: export default Button
   143→└── Models/
   144→    └── index.d.ts
   145→```
   146→
   147→**Models/index.d.ts**
   148→
   149→```ts
   150→export interface ButtonProps {
   151→  text: string;
   152→  disabled?: boolean;
   153→  onPress?: () => void;
   154→}
   155→export interface ButtonState {
   156→  pressed: boolean;
   157→}
   158→export interface ButtonHandlers {
   159→  handlePress: () => void;
   160→}
   161→export type UseButtonReturn = ButtonState & ButtonHandlers;
   162→```
   163→
   164→**index.ts (logic hook)**
   165→
   166→```ts
   167→import { useCallback, useState } from 'react';
   168→import type { ButtonProps, UseButtonReturn } from './Models';
   169→
   170→export function useButton(props: ButtonProps): UseButtonReturn {
   171→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);
   172→
   173→  const handlePress = useCallback(() => {
   174→    setPressed(true);
   175→    props.onPress?.();
   176→    setTimeout(() => setPressed(false), 150);
   177→  }, [props.onPress]);
   178→
   179→  return {
   180→    pressed,
   181→    handlePress,
   182→  };
   183→}
   184→```
   185→
   186→**view.tsx (render)**
   187→
   188→```tsx
   189→import React from 'react';
   190→import { Pressable, View } from 'react-native';
   191→import { Text } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
   192→import { useButton } from './index';
   193→import type { ButtonProps } from './Models';
   194→
   195→export default function Button(props: ButtonProps) {
   196→  const { pressed, handlePress } = useButton(props);
   197→
   198→  return (
   199→    <Pressable disabled={props.disabled} onPress={handlePress}>
   200→      <View>
   201→        <Text>
   202→          {props.text}
   203→          {pressed ? ' •' : ''}
   204→        </Text>
   205→      </View>
   206→    </Pressable>
   207→  );
   208→}
   209→```
   210→
   211→---
   212→
   213→## Project Organization
   214→
   215→```
   216→src/
   217→├── @types/            # Global TS types
   218→├── assets/            # Images, fonts, icons
   219→├── atomic/            # UI components (Hook Component pattern)
   220→│   ├── atoms/
   221→│   ├── molecules/
   222→│   └── organisms/
   223→├── modules/           # Microfrontends shared and native modules
   224→├── screens/           # Page-level (Hook Component pattern)
   225→├── services/          # Domain logic (SOLID layout)
   226→│   ├── api/           # HTTP layer (Axios/Fetch)
   227→│   ├── class/         # Class with logical requests
   228→│   ├── hooks/         # Specific service hooks
   229→│   ├── repository/    # Data abstraction & mapping
   230→│   ├── services/      # Business orchestration
   231→│   ├── storage/       # Storage orchestration with cache or not
   232→│   ├── store/         # Zustand slices (only)
   233→│   ├── models/        # Domain types & contracts
   234→│   ├── utils/         # Reusable service helps
   235→│   └── index.ts       # Public exports
   236→├── hooks/             # Cross-cutting React hooks
   237→├── routes/            # Navigation config
   238→├── settings/          # Env & global config (axios, etc.)
   239→└── utils/             # General-purpose helpers
   240→```
   241→
   242→> UI **never** calls API directly. All effects & side effects live in the **logic hooks** (which call `/services`).
   243→
   244→---
   245→
   246→## Services Architecture
   247→
   248→**Flow:** `UI → services → repository → api` and `UI → services → store (Zustand)`
   249→
   250→- **api/**: request clients; no domain logic.
   251→- **class/**: class with logical requests.
   252→- **hooks/**: specific service hooks.
   253→- **repository/**: maps transport to domain, aggregates sources.
   254→- **services/**: orchestration and business rules.
   255→- **storage/**: storage orchestration with cache or not.
   256→- **store/**: Zustand slices (single source of app state).
   257→- **models/**: types, DTOs, contracts.
   258→- **utils/**: reusable service helpers.
   259→
   260→Principles:
   261→
   262→- Single responsibility per folder/file.
   263→- No circular dependencies.
   264→- Public APIs exported via `services/index.ts` per domain.
   265→
   266→---
   267→
   268→## State Management (Zustand-only)
   269→
   270→- All global/shared state is **Zustand**.
   271→- Slices live under `/services/<domain>/store/`.
   272→- Use `persist` (with `createJSONStorage`) only when needed.
   273→- Keep setters pure; side effects go in hooks or services.
   274→
   275→**Slice example**
   276→
   277→```ts
   278→import type { StateCreator } from 'zustand';
   279→import type { AuthActions, AuthState } from '../models/store';
   280→
   281→export type AuthSlice = {
   282→  auth: AuthState;
   283→  authActions: AuthActions;
   284→};
   285→
   286→const initialAuth = {
   287→  user: null,
   288→  loading: false,
   289→  error: undefined,
   290→};
   291→
   292→export const createAuthSlice: StateCreator<
   293→  AuthSlice,
   294→  [],
   295→  [],
   296→  AuthSlice
   297→> = set => ({
   298→  auth: initialAuth,
   299→  authActions: {
   300→    setLoading: v =>
   301→      set(s => ({
   302→        auth: {
   303→          ...s.auth,
   304→          isLoading: v,
   305→        },
   306→      })),
   307→    setUser: v =>
   308→      set(s => ({
   309→        auth: {
   310→          ...s.auth,
   311→          user: v,
   312→        },
   313→      })),
   314→    setError: v =>
   315→      set(s => ({
   316→        auth: {
   317→          ...s.auth,
   318→          error: v,
   319→        },
   320→      })),
   321→    reset: () => set({ auth: initialAuth }),
   322→  },
   323→});
   324→```
   325→
   326→**Root Store**
   327→
   328→- Applies to ALL Sem Parar frontend apps — including SuperApp.
   329→
   330→```ts
   331→import { create } from 'zustand';
   332→import { devtools, persist, createJSONStorage } from 'zustand/middleware';
   333→import { immer } from 'zustand/middleware/immer';
   334→
   335→import {
   336→  createUserSlice,
   337→  type UserSlice,
   338→} from '@semparar/mf-app-user/src/newArch/user';
   339→import { createAuthSlice, type AuthSlice } from '@semparar/mf-app-yourmf';
   340→
   341→import storage from '~/redux/store/storage';
   342→
   343→/** Add your slice Type */
   344→export type RootStore = UserSlice & AuthSlice;
   345→
   346→export const useRootStore = create<RootStore>()(
   347→  devtools(
   348→    persist(
   349→      immer((...a) => ({
   350→        ...createUserSlice(...a),
   351→        ...createAuthSlice(...a), // Add your slice here
   352→      })),
   353→      {
   354→        name: 'root-storage',
   355→        storage: createJSONStorage(() => storage),
   356→        partialize: s => ({
   357→          user: s.user,
   358→          auth: s.auth, // Add your store data
   359→        }),
   360→        merge: (persisted, current) => ({
   361→          ...current,
   362→          ...(persisted as Partial<RootStore>),
   363→        }),
   364→      },
   365→    ),
   366→    { name: 'RootStore' },
   367→  ),
   368→);
   369→```
   370→
   371→- For Use on Class.
   372→
   373→```ts
   374→class Auth {
   375→  private readonly setAuth;
   376→
   377→  constructor () {
   378→    this.setUser = useRootStore.getState().authActions.setUser;
   379→  }
   380→
   381→  function doSomething(data) {
   382→    this.setUser({ ...data });
   383→  }
   384→}
   385→```
   386→
   387→- For Use on Component/Screen.
   388→- UI never imports the store directly — uses selectors
   389→
   390→```ts
   391→const loading = useRootStore(s => s.user.isLoading);
   392→const token = useRootStore(s => s.auth.token);
   393→```
   394→
   395→---
   396→
   397→## Routes Naming
   398→
   399→Standardize as **`Acronym + Route`**:
   400→
   401→- ✅ `SPPayment` (Sem Parar Payment)
   402→- ✅ `FTHPayment` (Fintech Payment)
   403→
   404→---
   405→
   406→## Folder Naming Rules
   407→
   408→| Folder                              | Convention   | Examples                   |
   409→| ----------------------------------- | ------------ | -------------------------- |
   410→| `atomic`, `screens`, `routes`       | `PascalCase` | `HomeScreen`, `CarDetails` |
   411→| `assets`, `hooks`, `services`, etc. | `camelCase`  | `useAuth`, `authService`   |
   412→
   413→---
   414→
   415→## Code Standards
   416→
   417→**Language**: English only.  
   418→**Naming**: `camelCase` (vars/functions), `PascalCase` (components/classes), `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` (constants).
   419→
   420→**Explanatory variables**
   421→
   422→```js
   423→const onlyNumbersRegex = /^\d+$/;
   424→const getNumberValidation = message => value =>
   425→  !onlyNumbersRegex.test(value) && message;
   426→const isNumber = getNumberValidation('error message');
   427→isNumber(1);
   428→```
   429→
   430→**Semantic constants**
   431→
   432→```js
   433→const DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS = 86400000;
   434→setTimeout(doSomething, DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS);
   435→```
   436→
   437→**Library workarounds with link**
   438→
   439→```js
   440→// Refer to issue https://github.com/ngokevin/react-file-reader-input/issues/11#issuecomment-363484861
   441→// eslint-disable-next-line no-param-reassign
   442→event.target.value = '';
   443→```
   444→
   445→**JSDoc**
   446→
   447→```js
   448→/**
   449→ * Truncates an event name to the max length.
   450→ * @param {*} eventName
   451→ * @param {*} maxLength
   452→ */
   453→```
   454→
   455→**Hooks: multi-line deps & IIFE**
   456→
   457→```ts
   458→useEffect(() => {
   459→  (async () => {
   460→    await CustomMath.randomSum();
   461→    setSum(42);
   462→  })();
   463→}, [setSum]);
   464→```
   465→
   466→**Extract conditions**
   467→
   468→```ts
   469→const hasSelectedOffer = !!(selectedProduct || selectedLeasingOffer);
   470→if (!hasSelectedOffer && !individualOffer) return;
   471→```
   472→
   473→**Optional chaining**
   474→
   475→```ts
   476→const answers = formikForm?.values?.answers;
   477→```
   478→
   479→**JSX indentation**
   480→
   481→```tsx
   482→{
   483→  !isEnd && <Button text="CTA" onPress={handleNavigate} />;
   484→}
   485→```
   486→
   487→**Early returns**
   488→
   489→```ts
   490→if (!data) return null;
   491→return <View />;
   492→```
   493→
   494→**Nullish coalescing**
   495→
   496→```ts
   497→return data ?? 0;
   498→```
   499→
   500→---
   501→
   502→## Styles Patterns
   503→
   504→- Do **not** use `StyleSheet` or `styled-components`.
   505→- Use **@semparar/design-system-app** for components, tokens, and theming.
   506→
   507→---
   508→
   509→## Cache Pattern
   510→
   511→Keep cache typed, serializable, and domain.
   512→
   513→```ts
   514→import storage from '~/redux/store/storage';
   515→
   516→export interface CachedData<T> {
   517→  timestamp: number;
   518→  data: T;
   519→}
   520→
   521→export interface StorageContract<T> {
   522→  get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null>;
   523→  save(data: T): Promise<void>;
   524→  remove(): Promise<void>;
   525→}
   526→
   527→export class GenericStorage<T> implements StorageContract<T> {
   528→  constructor(private readonly key: string) {}
   529→
   530→  async get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null> {
   531→    const json = await storage.getItem(this.key);
   532→    if (!json) return null;
   533→    return JSON.parse(json) as CachedData<T>;
   534→  }
   535→
   536→  async save(data: T): Promise<void> {
   537→    const payload: CachedData<T> = {
   538→      timestamp: Date.now(),
   539→      data,
   540→    };
   541→    await storage.setItem(this.key, JSON.stringify(payload));
   542→  }
   543→
   544→  async remove(): Promise<void> {
   545→    await storage.removeItem(this.key);
   546→  }
   547→}
   548→```
   549→
   550→**Guidelines**
   551→
   552→- One storage class per module/feature.
   553→- Types/interfaces live in `Models/` where appropriate.
   554→- Use `timestamp` for freshness/expiry strategies.
   555→- Never persist secrets/PII outside secure storage.
   556→- Keep payloads small and JSON-serializable.
   557→
   558→---
   559→
   560→## Checklist
   561→
   562→- [ ] Screens/Components follow **Hook Component pattern** (`index.ts` hook + `view.tsx` render)
   563→- [ ] Types live in **Models/**
   564→- [ ] Only **Zustand** for global/shared state under **/services/<domain>/store/**
   565→- [ ] UI never calls API directly (go through **/services**)
   566→- [ ] Code standards and styles patterns applied
   567→- [ ] Cache implemented via **GenericStorage** (when needed)
   568→- [ ] Routes and folder naming conventions followed
   569→
   570→---
   571→
   572→Happy shipping! 🚀
   573→

Perfect. Now let me create a comprehensive summary report based on my findings. --- ## SDUI Implementation Exploration Report I've thoroughly explored the SDUI implementation in the codebase. Here's what currently exists and what's missing: ### 1. Type System - COMPLETE (SP_0002.0) **File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/models/index.ts` **Key Exports:** - **Context & Interpolation (RFC 3.4)** - `SDUIContextValue` — recursive type for context values (string, number, boolean, null, object, array) - `SDUIContext` — dictionary of context values - **JSON Logic Subset (RFC 3.5)** - `SDUIVarOperator` — `{ var: 'path.to.value' }` for context access - `SDUIComparisonRule` — `==`, `!=`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=` operators - `SDUILogicalRule` — `and`, `or`, `!` operators - `SDUIRule` — union of comparison + logical rules - `SDUIConditional` — `{ rule: ... }` for visibility/enabled state - `SDUIValidationRule` — rule + error message - `SDUIValidation` — array of validation rules - **Rich Text (RFC 3.3)** - `SDUITextNode` — text with optional bold, italic, color - `SDUILinkNode` — link with text, target, optional trackEvent - `SDUIIconNode` — icon with name, color, size - `SDUILineBreakNode` — `
` equivalent - `SDUIRichText` — container for rich text children - **Components (RFC 3.2)** - `SDUIPropValue` — recursive prop type (primitives, conditionals, validations, rich text, nested objects/arrays) - `SDUIComponentProps` — dictionary of props - `SDUIComponent` — `{ type, props, visible? }` - **Screen Structure (RFC 3.1)** - `SDUIHeader` — component for header - `SDUIFooter` — component for footer - `SDUIScreen` — `{ id, context, header?, body[], footer? }` - `SDUIScreenResponse` — API envelope - **Component Registry** - `SDUIComponentRenderer

` — React ComponentType expecting `{ props, context }` - `SDUIComponentRegistry` — map of type → renderer component - `SDUI_KNOWN_TYPES` — const array of recognized component types: - `heading`, `text`, `balance_display`, `points_input`, `button`, `link`, `alert` - `list_text_icon`, `separator`, `icon_text_row`, `section_heading`, `nav_header`, `rich_text` ### 2. Directory Structure ``` src/services/sdui/ └── models/ └── index.ts (4,006 bytes — complete type system) ``` **Note:** Only the type definitions exist. No utility files yet. ### 3. Missing Implementations (Per Progress.txt) | Task | Status | Description | |------|--------|-------------| | **SP_0002.1** | PENDING | Interpolation parser — {{var}} replacement engine | | **SP_0002.2** | PENDING | Component registry & screen renderer — core rendering engine | | **SP_0002.3** | PENDING | Component adapters — bridge SDUI types to DS components | | **SP_0002.4** | PENDING | Mock JSON payloads — realistic test data (4 screens × 2 products) | | **SP_0002.5** | PENDING | Demo stories — final SDUI rendering proof in Storybook | ### 4. Existing Validation & Infrastructure **Stories (Storybook):** - `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/SmokeTest.stories.tsx` — Basic DS component proof - `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/DSComponentValidation.stories.tsx` — Component validation **Status:** 8/10 DS components render in Storybook web context: - **WORKS:** Text, Button, Box, InputBox, Alert, Icon, ListTextIcon, SeparatorHorizontal - **FAILS (need fallbacks):** BackDropBottom (Modalize), Loading (Lottie) ### 5. Key RFC Requirements (from `.claude/rfc-conteudo-dinamico.txt`) The RFC outlines exactly what needs implementing: ``` 6. Implementação 6.1. O que precisa ser feito - Registro de componentes (mapeamento tipo → componente) - Renderizador que itera o array e renderiza cada tipo - Parser de interpolação (substituir {{var}}) - Avaliador de regras (subset de JSON Logic) ``` ### 6. Architectural Context - **Service Layer Pattern:** UI → hooks → services → repository → API - **State Management:** Zustand slices only (no Redux/Context) - **Hook Component Pattern:** `index.ts` (logic) + `view.tsx` (render) + `Models/index.d.ts` (types) - **Export Path:** SDUI types not yet exported from `src/services/index.ts` ### 7. Next Steps (From Progress Tracker) The spike targets **voucher screens** (SPMAIS-34653) with 4 variations: 1. Voucher form (points/reais input) 2. Voucher summary (review before confirm) 3. Voucher conclusion (success) 4. Regras de uso (rules) **Each step depends on the previous:** - SP_0002.1 (interpolation) feeds into SP_0002.2 (renderer) - SP_0002.2 + SP_0002.3 (adapters) combine to render screens - SP_0002.4 provides test payloads - SP_0002.5 demonstrates the entire pipeline in Storybook --- ## Summary **What Exists:** - Complete TypeScript type system for SDUI (180 lines of well-structured types) - Comprehensive RFC documentation - Storybook infrastructure with 8/10 DS components validated - Progress tracking and task breakdown **What's Missing:** - Interpolation utility ({{var}} parser) - Component registry + screen renderer - Component adapters (SDUI type → DS component mappings) - Mock JSON payloads - Storybook demo stories **Files to Reference:** - `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/models/index.ts` — Type definitions - `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/.claude/rfc-conteudo-dinamico.txt` — RFC (Portuguese, detailed) - `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/.claude/progress.txt` — Task breakdown
